data.title: Spain and India Strengthen Economic and Technological Collaboration
data.description: Spain and India have committed to enhancing their economic and technological partnership amid growing global competition in artificial intelligence. Bloomberg posted on X that this decision was made during a bilateral meeting between Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Wednesday. The leaders discussed strategies to bolster cooperation in these sectors, reflecting the increasing importance of AI in the global market.

data.title: Milo Surpasses $100 Million in Crypto-Backed Loan Originations
data.description: Milo, a lender specializing in crypto-backed mortgages, has achieved a milestone by surpassing $100 million in total loan originations. According to NS3.AI, this includes a record-setting single loan of $12 million. The company offers loans secured by Bitcoin or Ethereum, enabling clients to purchase homes, acquire land, undertake renovations, and invest in businesses without liquidating their crypto assets. Milo operates in 10 U.S. states, with interest rates beginning at 8.25%.

data.title: India Faces Decision on Russian Oil Tanker Insurance
data.description: The Indian government is approaching a critical decision regarding the continuation of allowing a select number of Russian companies to provide insurance for tankers delivering oil to India. Bloomberg posted on X that this decision comes amid ongoing geopolitical tensions and economic considerations. The outcome could significantly impact India's energy supply chain and its diplomatic relations with Russia. The decision is expected to be made in the coming days, reflecting the complex interplay of international trade and national policy.

data.title: Successful Fundraising Attributed to Strategic Investments in Key Projects
data.description: Rob Hadick, a general partner, highlighted the primary reason for the successful fundraising as the strategic and timely investment in leading projects across various sectors. According to ChainCatcher, these investments include the prediction market platform Polymarket and the stablecoin debit card issuer Rain.

data.title: Phoenix Secures $30.2 Million for Rare-Earth Refining Expansion
data.description: Phoenix, a rare-earth refiner, has successfully raised $30.2 million from investors to enhance its facilities dedicated to extracting essential minerals from mining waste. Bloomberg posted on X that the funding round included participation from metals trader Traxys. The investment will support Phoenix's efforts to expand its capabilities in recovering valuable materials, crucial for various industries. This development highlights the growing interest in sustainable mining practices and the importance of rare-earth elements in modern technology.

data.title: Binance founder CZ unfollowed EnHeng.
data.description: According to market sources, Binance founder CZ has unfollowed EnHeng.

data.title: Dragonfly Capital completes fundraising for its fourth fund of 650 million yuan, with early investments in Polymarket and Rain.
data.description: Dragonfly Capital announced the completion of its fourth fund raising, totaling $650 million. Rob Hadick, a partner at Dragonfly Capital, stated that this round of funding benefited from early investments in projects such as Polymarket and Rain. Dragonfly Capital was co-founded in 2018 by Alex Pack and Bo Feng, and is currently led by Haseeb Qureshi and Tom Schmidt. Tom Schmidt pointed out that the token form in the crypto industry is shifting from application-native tokens to tokenized products representing real-world assets, and that the growth of digital native currencies from zero to trillions in scale within 10 years is a long-term structural trend.

data.title: BlackRock deposited 2494.6 BTC, worth $168 million, into Coinbase.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, BlackRock (intel.arkm...blackrock) deposited 2494.6 BTC, worth $168.39 million, into Coinbase, and may continue to deposit in the future.

data.title: Employment Insurance Initiative for Indian Women Gains Support from Hillary Clinton
data.description: Approximately 500,000 Indian women now benefit from employment insurance designed to protect against extreme heat conditions. Bloomberg posted on X that Hillary Clinton has endorsed this initiative, aiming to enhance workplace safety and security for women in India. The program seeks to address the growing concerns related to climate change and its impact on vulnerable populations, particularly women who are disproportionately affected by extreme weather conditions. By providing insurance coverage, the initiative aims to mitigate the risks associated with heat exposure, ensuring that women can continue to work safely and sustainably. This development marks a significant step towards improving labor conditions and promoting gender equality in the workforce.

data.title: Uber Invests Over $100 Million in Autonomous Vehicle Charging Stations
data.description: Uber is set to invest more than $100 million in the construction of fast-charging stations for autonomous vehicles across the United States. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this initiative as part of Uber's strategy to strengthen its position in the robotaxi sector. The company aims to enhance infrastructure to support the growing demand for autonomous transportation solutions. This investment reflects Uber's commitment to advancing technology and innovation in the transportation industry, as it seeks to become a key player in the evolving market of self-driving vehicles.

data.title: Germany Rules Out Developing Nuclear Weapons
data.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has dismissed the possibility of Germany developing its own nuclear arsenal. Bloomberg posted on X that Merz proposed an alternative approach, suggesting that Germany could deploy nuclear bombs from France and the United Kingdom instead. This statement comes amid ongoing discussions about Europe's defense strategy and nuclear capabilities. Merz's comments highlight Germany's stance on nuclear armament and its reliance on allied nations for strategic defense measures.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | U.S. Prioritizes National Security in Greenland
data.description: The Trump administration's focus on Greenland is primarily driven by national security concerns, according to energy chief Chris Wright. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the interest is not centered on the development of Greenland's rare earths or energy resources. This statement clarifies the administration's strategic priorities in the region.

data.title: GS Reports Market Stress with Volatility Index at 9
data.description:
Macro Charts posted on X, GS reports market stress with its vol stress index at 9 out of 10. Historically, such readings are buying opportunities, but this time feels different.

The panic increase hasn't decreased spot prices, treating it as an equity-specific and model-specific issue. There's little panic in credit spreads, bond market volatility, or liquidity.

Without a clear risk-off catalyst, there's no defined scenario to fix the problem. Single-stock volatility may persist, raising concerns about its impact on the index and wider macro markets.

data.title: Amazon Faces Privacy Concerns Over Ring's New Surveillance Feature
data.description:
Amazon's Ring has introduced a new feature called Search Party, which has sparked privacy concerns among users. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the backlash from privacy advocates who argue that the feature could lead to increased surveillance and potential misuse of personal data. The Search Party feature allows users to share video footage with law enforcement agencies, raising questions about privacy and data security.

Critics are worried that this could lead to unwarranted surveillance and infringe on individual privacy rights. Amazon has defended the feature, stating that it is designed to enhance community safety and assist in criminal investigations. However, privacy experts caution that the potential for abuse and lack of transparency in data handling could undermine user trust.

The controversy comes amid growing scrutiny over tech companies' handling of personal data and their role in surveillance. As debates over privacy and security continue, Amazon's Ring faces pressure to address these concerns and ensure that user data is protected.

data.title: Digital euro timeline eyed amid ECB succession rumors
data.description:
FT cites early-exit talk as EU explores successor; an ECB official says no news. Christine Lagarde resignation, ECB succession, digital euro timeline.
source: https://coincu.com/news/digital-euro-timeline-eyed-amid-ecb-succession-rumors/

data.title: NilChain will cease operation on March 23, prompting holders to migrate NIL to Ethereum.
data.description: Nillion announced on the X platform that NilChain will cease operation on March 23. Users holding NIL on Cosmos must immediately migrate to Ethereum. Standard transfers will be unavailable after the shutdown.

data.title: Key Economic Indicators Scheduled for Release Today
data.description: A series of significant economic data releases are expected today, including Durable Goods, Housing Starts, Industrial Production, and Leading Indicators. Bespoke Investment Group posted on X. These reports are anticipated to provide insights into the current state of the economy and may influence market trends. Analysts and investors are closely monitoring these indicators to assess economic health and potential impacts on financial markets.

data.title: ETHZilla Stock Drops Over 8% Following Founders Fund's Stake Sale
data.description: Peter Thiel and his Founders Fund have sold their entire 7.5% stake in ETHZilla, a corporate Ethereum treasury company. According to NS3.AI, this move led to an over 8% decline in ETHZilla's stock price. The company, which shifted its focus from biotech to acquiring Ethereum tokens, has experienced a significant stock price drop of over 97% since its peak in August. Despite these losses, ETHZilla remains committed to advancing its tokenization projects, which include initiatives in home loans and commercial jet engines.

data.title: Glencore CEO Advocates for Larger Mining Companies Amid Political Challenges
data.description: Glencore CEO Gary Nagle emphasized the need for major mining companies to expand in order to amplify their influence in the industry. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Nagle's remarks on the growing political significance of metals and commodities. As these resources become more entwined with global politics, Nagle believes that larger mining entities will be better positioned to navigate the complexities and assert their interests effectively. This perspective underscores the strategic importance of scale in the mining sector as it faces evolving geopolitical dynamics.
